title: Paris municipal elections: Emmanuel Grégoire and the united left achieve a resounding victory
subhead: Despite a united front from the entire right-wing and centrist parties – as well as competition on the left with La France Insoumise candidate Sophia Chikirou remaining in the race – the Socialist candidate delivered the united left its most impressive victory in Paris since 2001.

title: French ex-PM Lionel Jospin dies aged 88
subhead: Jospin, a Socialist who introduced the 35-hour work week and civil partnerships for gay couples, was head of government from 1997 to 2002 before losing to the far right in presidential polls.

title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to apply provisionally from May 1
subhead: The pact still needs a green light from lawmakers in the European Parliament, which referred it to the EU's top court. But the European Commission has opted for a provisional application.

title: French municipal elections: Full results in every city, town and village
subhead: French voters elected more than 34,000 mayors on March 15 and 22. Use our search engine to find the winner in any municipality.

title: Vladimir Putin, the conductor of a 'special musical operation' in Europe
subhead: Barred from the West, Valery Gergiev, maestro at the helm of Russia's two leading opera houses and cultural ambassador for Vladimir Putin, is seeking a return to conducting in Europe's major concert halls. In this cultural arena, Moscow is shifting from 'soft power' to 'sharp power.'

title: The UAE reorganizes its arms supply network for Sudanese paramilitaries
subhead: Abu Dhabi is seeking to develop new logistical routes from Sudan's neighboring countries, such as Ethiopia and the Central African Republic, to support the Rapid Support Forces.
